title: React Lifecycle Methods Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of mounting, updating, and unmounting phases in React components.

questions.question: Which phase of the React lifecycle is associated with a component being created and inserted into the DOM?
options: Mounting
Updating
Unmounting
Initializing

questions.question: In functional components, how do you handle side effects on mount and unmount?
options: By setting window.onLoad and window.onUnload callbacks.
By using the useEffect() hook with a cleanup function and empty dependency array.
By calling getLifecycleMethods() inside the function body.
Only by using class-based syntax.

questions.question: When does the 'Updating' phase occur in the React lifecycle?
options: In a single-page application upon initial load.
When a component's state or props change.
When a user navigates to a new page.
When the battery level is low.

questions.question: How do you perform 'cleanup' (e.g., canceling a timer) in a functional component using useEffect?
options: By calling useCleanup() at the end.
By returning a function from within the useEffect() hook.
By setting cleanup=true in props.
None of the above.

questions.question: Does useEffect() with an EMPTY ([]) dependency array behave like componentDidMount in class components?
options: Yes, it runs only once after the initial render.
No, it runs after every update.
Yes, but only in ES6 or later.
No, it is an object in older browsers.
